commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=46109a6a71ea800b0d037244a16cd7f34b9c93f5)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Telegram isolated ingress timeout recovery to use a shorter timeout, release timed-out spooled claims back to pending with `last_error`, and update the related tests.

PR surface: Source -3, Tests +5. Total +2 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: the PR requeues the claim before handler settlement, while the existing token-guarded cleanup will no-op after the row is pending, leaving it available for a later retry. I did not run a live Telegram repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the PR has a correctness blocker and the external contributor still needs to provide real behavior proof that automation cannot supply for them.

target.title: fix(telegram): release stuck ingress claims on timeout instead of dead-lettering
target.body:
## Summary

When a Telegram ingress handler hangs during event processing (e.g., model API timeout), the event claim is held indefinitely. The worker remains stuck with `attempts=0` and `last_error=null`, blocking all subsequent messages on the same lane (chat). The only recovery was manual SQLite intervention.

This change reduces the handler timeout from 25 minutes to 5 minutes, and changes the timeout action from **dead-lettering** (permanent failure) to **releasing** the claim so the event is retried with an incremented attempts counter.

Fixes #95350

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Prevent permanent deadlock of Telegram ingress processing when a handler claim hangs due to unresponsive model API or internal deadlock.

**Real environment tested**: Linux x86_64 / Ubuntu 24.04 / Node 24 / OpenClaw main

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/telegram-ingress-spool.test.ts && pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts`

**After-fix evidence**: Unit test output below

```bash
$ pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/telegram-ingress-spool.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  9 passed (9)

$ pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  59 passed (59)
```

**Observed result after the fix**: Handler timeouts now release the claim back to the pending queue with an incremented `attempts` counter and a descriptive `last_error`, allowing automatic retry. The handler timeout is detected after 5 minutes (down from 25).

**What was not tested**: End-to-end reproduction of the original 2-hour-triggered deadlock is not deterministic per the issue reporter. The fix is validated through the existing handler-timeout test suite which exercises the exact code path (claim → hang → detect → release → retry).

## Tests and validation

### Unit tests

```
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  59 passed (59)
```

Key tests updated:
- `releases a timed-out spooled handler for retry and restarts before draining later same-lane updates`
- `keeps a timed-out lane guarded until the old handler stops`
- `does not drain more updates on the old bot while a timeout restart is pending`
- `keeps a timed-out lane guarded when its release for retry cannot be written`
- `marks isolated ingress unhealthy when a spooled backlog handler times out`
- `recovers a lone active spooled handler owned by a replaced session`

## Risk checklist

- [x] This change is backwards compatible
- [x] This change has been tested with existing configurations
- [ ] I have updated relevant documentation — N/A (behavioral patch, no new config surface)
- [ ] Breaking changes (if any) are documented in Summary — No breaking changes

## Current review state

- [x] Self-review completed
- [ ] At least one maintainer review
- [ ] All CI checks passed
